source_section.title_label_raw: General Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning

sections.title_label_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: For any new development over 2,000 square metres of floor area in any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, or HR-1 zone, the applicant other than a not-for-profit organization shall be required to provide incentive or bonus zoning. (RC-May 23/24;E-Jun 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: For any development agreement in accordance with Policies CH-7, ED-6, F-4, F-5, UD-13, IM-35.6, IM-37, IM-38, IM-39, IM- 40, IM-41, IM-42, IM-43, IM-44, IM-45, or IM-46 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy, the applicant shall provide incentive or bonus zoning. (RC-Sep 9/25;E-Oct 3/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: Where an applicant is required to provide incentive or bonus zoning in accordance with either Subsection 472(1) or Subsection 472(2), public benefits shall be provided as per the requirements of Section 479.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: Where an applicant provides incentive or bonus zoning in accordance with Subsection 472(1), the Development Officer may require the applicant to enter into an incentive or bonus zoning agreement.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: Where an applicant provides incentive or bonus zoning in accordance with Subsection 472(2), the Development Officer may require the applicant to enter into an incentive or bonus zoning agreement. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Incentive or Bonus Zoning Requirements Requirement to Provide a Public Benefit for Incentive or Bonus Zoning
provisions.text: Where an incentive or bonus zoning agreement is required under Subsections 472(4) or 472(5), no development permit shall be issued until the agreement is executed by all the parties and filed in the Provincial Land Registration Office. Calculation of the Public Benefit Value for a Development Exceeding 2,000 Square Metres in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, or HR-1 Zone

sections.title_label_raw: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, or HR-1 zone, the public

provisions.heading_context_raw: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, or HR-1 zone, the public
provisions.text: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, or HR-1 zone, the public benefit value required under section 472 is calculated using the following formula PBV = 0.2(NFA - 2000)(BR) in which PBV = public benefit value in dollars NFA = new floor area in square metres BR = bonus rate in dollars per square metre as provided in Section 475 (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) Deadline to Complete Public Benefit

sections.title_label_raw: An applicant shall complete a public benefit as described in Section

provisions.heading_context_raw: An applicant shall complete a public benefit as described in Section
provisions.text: An applicant shall complete a public benefit as described in Section 472 before a certificate of occupancy is issued. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E- Feb 9/24) Incentive or Bonus Zoning Rates

sections.title_label_raw: The bonus rates for the districts identified on Schedule 50 are

provisions.heading_context_raw: The bonus rates for the districts identified on Schedule 50 are
provisions.text: The bonus rates for the districts identified on Schedule 50 are set out for Districts 1 through 6 as of April 2023 in Table 18. (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24) Table 18: Incentive or bonus zoning rates and districts Bonus rate district # Name of bonus rate district Bonus rate, 2023 (per square metre) 1 South End Halifax (including Downtown Halifax) $299.20 2 Cogswell Redevelopment Lands $299.20 3 North End Halifax $208.74 4 North Dartmouth $97.41 5 Downtown and Central Dartmouth $166.99 6 Woodside $76.54

provisions.heading_context_raw: The bonus rates for the districts identified on Schedule 50 are
provisions.text: The bonus rates in Table 18 shall be adjusted annually in accordance with Section 477. Incentive or Bonus Zoning for Future Growth Nodes

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each Future Growth Node that is covered by a CDD-2 or CDD-1 zone shall be determined, based on the appraised market value of the site once the proposed development agreement is approved by Council, and then multiplied by a coefficient of 0.12.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: The appraised market value is not the market value of the completed project, but the value that the applicant could expect to receive if they sold the site with the development agreement in place.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: Any appraisal under this section shall be completed by an appraiser that is hired by the Municipality.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: The cost of any appraisal shall be paid for by the applicant.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: The terms of reference for the appraisal shall be determined solely by the Municipality.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: Where the Municipality or applicant disagrees with the appraised value determined in the appraisal, the disagreeing party may, at the applicant's expense, have a second appraisal done, and the appraisal is subject to the same terms of reference as the initial appraisal.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: The appraised value for the purposes of the public benefit value is:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: where there is one appraisal, the monetary value of the land from that appraisal; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 476(2), the public benefit value for each
provisions.text: where there are two appraisals, the average monetary values of the land from the two appraisals. Incentive or Bonus Zoning Rate Adjustments

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 477(2), before being used to calculate a

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 477(2), before being used to calculate a
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 477(2), before being used to calculate a required public benefit value, the bonus rates specified in Table 18 shall be adjusted annually on April 1st in accordance with changes to the Halifax All-Items Consumer Price Index released by Statistics Canada. Rates shall be adjusted using the method specified in Appendix 3.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 477(2), before being used to calculate a
provisions.text: If the Halifax All-Items Consumer Price Index declines or remains unchanged in a given year, there shall be no change in the bonus rates for that year.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 477(2), before being used to calculate a
provisions.text: The bonus rate for the calculation of the required public benefit value shall be the bonus rate at the time a complete application for a development permit is received by the Municipality. Proposal for Required Public Benefits

sections.title_label_raw: An applicant who is required to provide a public benefit shall submit a

provisions.heading_context_raw: An applicant who is required to provide a public benefit shall submit a
provisions.text: An applicant who is required to provide a public benefit shall submit a public benefit value calculation, as detailed in Section 473, and public benefits proposal with their application for a development permit, in a format acceptable to the Development Officer. Public Benefit Categories

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is required to provide for incentive or bonus zoning shall provide money-in-lieu for affordable housing and in addition shall provide one or a combination of the following public benefits:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: additional money-in-lieu for affordable housing;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: the conservation of a registered heritage building on the development site;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: money-in-lieu for the conservation of a registered heritage building that is not located on the development site;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: the conservation of a building within a heritage conservation district on the development site;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: money-in-lieu for the conservation of a building within a heritage conservation district that is not located on the development site;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: if agreed by the Municipality, improvements to municipal parks, municipal right-of-way, or registered heritage properties owned by the Municipality, that abut that development sites; (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: money-in-lieu for the acquisition or improvement of municipal parks;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: money-in-lieu for affordable community or cultural indoor space;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: money-in-lieu for public art; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 480(1) and 481(1), an applicant who is
provisions.text: public art on the development site. Public Benefit Requirement: Money-in-Lieu for Affordable Housing

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the total required public benefit value that shall be allocated to money-in-lieu for affordable housing is 60%.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: Money-in-lieu accepted for affordable housing shall be required to be used within the Municipality for:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: the rehabilitation of existing affordable housing units, including building assessments, provided by a not-for- profit organization or registered Canadian charitable organization;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: the acquisition of buildings, housing units, or properties for affordable housing;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: the creation of new affordable housing units by a not-for- profit organization or registered Canadian charitable organization;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: a housing agreement permitted in Clause 73(b) of the Charter; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 481(1), the minimum proportion of the
provisions.text: any combination of Clauses 480(2)(a) to 480(2)(d). Public Benefit Requirement: Conservation of a Registered Heritage Building or a Building within a Heritage Conservation District

sections.title_label_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a heritage conservation district:

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: 90% of the total required public benefit value shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: allocated to the conservation of a registered heritage building that is on the development site,

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: allocated to the conservation of a building within a heritage conservation district that is on the development site,

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: paid as money-in-lieu for the conservation of registered heritage properties that are not on the development site, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: paid as money-in-lieu for the conservation of properties within a heritage conservation district that are not on the development site; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: the remaining 10% of the total required public benefit value shall be allocated as money-in-lieu for affordable housing.

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a registered heritage property or on a property within a
provisions.text: The applicant shall register a waiver on title of the property that, without the approval of the Municipality, the registered heritage property or the property within a heritage conservation district shall not be altered or demolished under Section 18 of the Heritage Property Act. Public Benefit Requirement: On-Site Public Art

sections.title_label_raw: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:
provisions.text: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:
provisions.text: be located on the development site, and allow direct public access or viewing of the public art;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:
provisions.text: be designed by a professional artist; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Where provided as a public benefit on-site, public art shall:
provisions.text: have a minimum cost of $100,000. Unacceptable Forms of Public Art

sections.title_label_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art under the incentive or bonus zoning program:

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: interpretive, wayfinding, or other functional signage;

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: branding or promotional projects;

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: plaques and supporting infrastructure;

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: stock and mass-produced items;

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: memorials, where:

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: the memorial commemorates a single individual not previously approved through the Municipality's Commemorative Asset Naming Program,

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: the memorial has not been designed and created by a professional artist, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: the primary component or element of design involves benches, picnic tables, playgrounds or other park infrastructure, trees, or other ornamental landscape elements; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: The following items do not qualify as acceptable forms of public art
provisions.text: landscape design, landscape gardens, or any garden features including fountains, garden furnishings, or other infrastructure, unless those elements have been conceived of by a professional artist independently or in collaboration with other design professionals and are deemed to be an integral component of an artwork. Incentive or Bonus Zoning Agreement

sections.title_label_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms respecting:

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: the identification of the development site;

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: design drawings, provided by the applicant, for any required or provided public benefit;

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: where required by the Development Officer, detailed construction drawings, site plans, specifications, cost estimates, or appraisals prepared by an appraiser for any required or provided public benefit;

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: the identification of any conditions required by the Municipality before the public benefit is accepted;

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: where required, provisions for the auditing and reporting of public benefits; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: any other terms or conditions the Development Officer requires.

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall be signed by the owner.

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 484(4) and 484(5), and in accordance with Section 31A of the Charter, Council delegates to the Development Officer the authority to:

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: Enter into an incentive or bonus zoning agreement, or an amendment to an incentive or bonus zoning agreement, on behalf of the Municipality; and (RC- Jul 12/22; E- Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: The authority:

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: discharge an incentive or bonus zoning agreement in whole or in part, in accordance with the terms of the incentive or bonus zoning agreement or with the concurrence of the property owner, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: sign the discharge, including a discharge agreement, on behalf of the Municipality. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: In accordance with Subsection 31A(5) of the Charter, where an incentive or bonus zoning agreement entered into by the Development Officer commits the Municipality to any expenditure, the agreement has no force or effect until approved by Council.

provisions.heading_context_raw: An incentive or bonus zoning agreement shall contain terms
provisions.text: In accordance with Subsection 31A(4) of the Charter, an incentive or bonus zoning agreement entered into by the Development Officer, or an amendment to such an agreement, shall be signed by the Mayor and the Municipal Clerk on behalf of the Municipality. Agreements
